title: Coryphantha recurvata
taxonomy: Coryphantha recurvata is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Coryphantha and the subgenus Pediocactus. The species is classified under the subgenus Pediocactus, which includes various species of small, globular cacti. Coryphantha recurvata is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The species is further classified under the binomial nomenclature, Coryphantha recurvata, which is widely accepted in botanical classifications.
uses: Coryphantha recurvata has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Mexico and the southwestern United States. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's extracts have been used to treat various ailments, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Additionally, Coryphantha recurvata has been used to make a paste for treating skin irritations and burns. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications and effectiveness.
